docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Documentation Style Guide
docs.text: This guide ensures consistency across all MujinOS documentation.

docs.title: Product Terminology
docs.text: Term Do Not Use Context MujinOS Mujin OS, mujin os Our primary operating system. Robotic Arm Robot, Bot Refer to the physical hardware precisely. Teaching Pendant Remote, Controller The handheld HMI device.

docs.title: Voice and Tone
docs.text:
• Direct and Active: Use "Press the button" instead of "The button should be pressed."
  • Safety First: Safety instructions must precede the step they describe.

docs.title: API & SDK Reference
docs.text:
MujinMI API Overview

The MujinMI (Machine Intelligence) API is the primary gateway for developers to interact with the MujinOS environment. It allows for high-level motion planning, real-time perception queries, and robot state management.

🔑 Authentication

All API requests must be authenticated. When using the Python SDK, provide your API key during controller initialization.

Header: X-Mujin-API-Key

Scope: Ensure your key has Write permissions for motion commands.

🚀 Quick Start: Python SDK

The easiest way to get started is by using our native Python wrapper. It abstracts the underlying REST/gRPC calls into simple method signatures.

import mujin_sdk

docs.title: Access a specific robotic arm
docs.text:
robot = controller.get_robot("primary_arm")

🛠 Python SDK Reference (Auto-generated)

The following section is synchronized directly with our src/ directory.

RobotController Class

💡 Best Practices

Error Handling: Always wrap motion commands in a try-except block.

Safety First: Commands are subject to hardware safety limits.

docs.title: src.mujin_interface.RobotController
docs.text:
The primary interface for controlling robotic arms within MujinOS.

Source code in src/mujin_interface.py 
class RobotController:
    """
    The primary interface for controlling robotic arms within MujinOS.
    """

    def connect(self, ip_address: str) -> bool:
        """
        Establishes a connection to the MujinController.

        Args:
            ip_address: The static IP of the physical controller.

        Returns:
            True if connection is successful, False otherwise.
        """
        # Simulated connection logic
        return True

    def move_to_angles(self, angles: list[float], speed: float = 0.5) -> bool:
        """
        Commands the robot to move to a specific set of joint angles.

        Args:
            angles: A list of 6 floats representing joint positions in radians.
            speed: Motion velocity (0.0 to 1.0).

        Returns:
            True if motion is planned and executed safely.
        """
        # Simulated motion planning
        return True

docs.title: Safety & Compliance Standards
docs.text:
The MujinController operates heavy industrial machinery. All integrators must adhere to ISO 10218-1 safety requirements.

Immediate Physical Hazard

Never enter the robot's work envelope while the "Auto" light is illuminated. Failure to comply can result in severe injury or death.

docs.title: Safety Layers
docs.text:
1. Mechanical: Hard stops and safety fencing.
  2. Electronic: Dual-channel Emergency Stop (E-Stop).
  3. Software: MujinOS real-time collision avoidance.
